name: incremental-implementation

positive_triggers.input: Add user CRUD operations to the app
Migrate the API from REST to GraphQL
Implement a checkout flow with cart, payment, confirmation
positive_triggers.reason: 3+ steps, multiple capabilities, needs vertical slicing

negative_triggers.input: Fix one line in login.ts that has a typo
negative_triggers.reason: Single-file trivial change, no slicing needed

negative_triggers.input: Update the version field in package.json
Show me the git log of the last 10 commits

expected_behavior: Agent decomposes task into vertical slices (not horizontal layers)
Slices are: create-user, read-user, update-user, delete-user (each end-to-end)
Each slice has net diff ≤ 100 lines
Agent uses git worktree or feature branch for isolation
Each slice gets own commit with 'slice:' prefix
Each slice is followed by test-driven-development verification
Agent updates progress.md with [slice] log lines
Agent decomposes by resource/capability, not by file type
First slice is read-only (add GraphQL read for one resource alongside REST)
Each slice ships independently (REST still works until all migrated)
Agent rehearses rollback: 'Can I revert just slice 3?' before declaring done

notes: Behavioral evals are warning-level until promoted via upstream issue tracker.
